documents.title: Addis Ababa City Administration And Civil Status 39057Ebe
documents.content:
The Addis Ababa City Administration is the executive organ of Ethiopia’s capital. A mayor heads the administration; the lowest unit is the woreda, which has an elected council.
Civil status services (birth, marriage and death registration) are provided at the woreda level. A birth certificate is obtained by registering a child's birth with the woreda, and a residence identity card is also issued at the same desk. The residence ID is required before obtaining a national passport.

documents.title: Anbessa City Bus Routes 4136911E
documents.content:
Anbessa City Bus Service Enterprise is a state‑owned operator in Addis Ababa. It was founded in 1945 and reorganised as a public enterprise in 1994.
Key facts:
- Network size: The network consists of 115 active bus routes.
- Route information: Each route listing includes the bus number, origin, pass‑by stops, destination and distance. Example: route 1 runs from Megenagna to Gurd Shola via Kara (7.7 km), route 2 runs from Kore Mekanisa to Addis Ketema via Lideta (11.1 km), route 3 runs from Ayertena to Minilik Square via Mexico (10.8 km).
- Usage: Passengers should consult the route table to find the correct bus number and distance before travelling.

documents.title: Birth Registration And Certificate Fdba9A8A
documents.content:
The Civil Registration and Residency Services Agency registers a child's birth at the woreda branch office and issues a birth certificate. The service covers live births and live births followed by immediate death; stillbirths are not registered.
Eligibility:
- Proxy registration: If both parents are alive but unable to register the birth due to force majeure, they may appoint a special birth proxy to the child's biological mother. If the mother is unable to register, the proxy may be given to the biological father.
- Death of a parent: When one parent has died, the living parent must present the legal death certificate of the deceased parent.
- Persons with disabilities: People with disabilities may register through their caregivers or guardians.
- Age of applicant: A person aged 18 or older must register their own birth. Parents who had a child before turning 18 may register the birth by submitting a support letter from their local lower administration office.
- Foreign nationals: The registrant must have been born in Ethiopia.
Required documents:
- Birth notification form from a health facility for births at a health facility.
- Proof of guardianship if a guardian registers the child.
- Legal identification or proof of identity when the police or government agency registers an abandoned child.
- Proof from a health institution when the registrant is a foreign citizen.
Notes: Residence IDs are not issued or renewed by proxy; diaspora Ethiopians and foreign nationals living abroad do not receive residence IDs; a new residence ID is issued after the service period expires.

documents.title: Birth Registration Service
documents.content:
# Birth Registration Service - Addis Ababa

## What is Birth Registration?
Birth registration is the official recording of a child's birth with the Civil Registration and Residency Services Agency (CRRSA). Every child born in Ethiopia has the right to a birth certificate.

## Where to Register
Visit the Civil Registration and Residency Services Agency branch office in your woreda (district).

## Eligibility
- All live births in Addis Ababa must be registered
- Parents can register their child if they are 18 years or older
- Proxy registration is allowed if parents cannot register due to force majeure

## Required Documents

### If Birth Occurred in Health Facility
- Birth notification form from the health facility

### If Birth Occurred at Home
- Written statement from the mother or father

### For Abandoned Children
- Legal identification from police or government agency

### For Foreign Citizens
- Document issued by health institution

### For Children with Guardians
- Proof of guardianship document

## Fees
- Current Registration: 60 Birr (Ethiopian citizens), 600 Birr (foreign citizens)
- Late Registration: 150 Birr (Ethiopian citizens), 750 Birr (foreign citizens)
- Expired Registration: 600 Birr (Ethiopian citizens), 900 Birr (foreign citizens)

## Important Notes
- Birth certificates cannot be issued by proxy
- Parents must register within the first year of birth
- Late registrations may require additional investigation

## Contact Information
- Phone: 7533
- Email: info@aacrrsa.gov.et
- Website: www.aacrrsa.gov.et

documents.title: Broken Streetlight Guide
documents.content:
Broken Streetlight Reporting Guide

Citizens can report a broken streetlight through the municipal infrastructure complaint desk.
Required details include the street name, nearest landmark, and a short description of the issue.
Reports can be submitted during office hours or through the official city complaint portal.
documents.chunk_text:

Reports can be submitted during office hours or through the official city complaint portal.

documents.title: Business Licence Application 77192Ff5
documents.content:
Businesses in Ethiopia must obtain a trade or business licence before operating and renew it periodically.
Steps:
1. Prerequisites – Obtain a Commercial Registration Certificate, Tax Identification Number (TIN) and a completed application form; other documents may be required based on business type.
2. Fill out the application form – Complete the physical or online form for a trade/business licence.
3. Prepare required documents – Gather the commercial registration certificate, TIN certificate and any other business‑specific documents.
4. Submit the application – Submit the form and documents to the relevant government office (e.g., Ministry of Trade or sub‑city trade bureau).
5. Obtain the licence – After review, the licence is issued; processing time and fees vary.
Renewal: Licences must be renewed periodically (often annually or semi‑annually). Applicants should follow the renewal timeline and ensure tax obligations are up to date.

documents.title: Business License Service
documents.content:
# Business License - Addis Ababa

## Overview
To operate a business in Addis Ababa, you need a business license from the Trade and Regional Integration Bureau.

## Where to Apply
Online: https://etrade.gov.et (Trade Portal)
In Person: Trade and Regional Integration Bureau offices

## Steps to Get Business License
1. Create account on trade portal
2. Register business name (get commercial registration)
3. Apply for business license
4. Submit required documents
5. Pay fees
6. Download certificate

## Required Documents

### For Sole Proprietorship
- Tax Identification Number (TIN)
- Passport-size photos (2)
- Copy of ID (passport, driver's license, or kebele ID)
- If using representative: power of attorney + ID

### For Private Limited Company
- Certificate of Incorporation
- Memorandum and Article of Association
- Tax Identification Number (TIN)
- Minutes of resolution
- List of shareholders
- Company ID or registration certificate

## Fees
Fees vary based on business type and size. Check the trade portal for specific fees.

## Important
- Business license must be displayed in your shop/office
- License must be renewed annually

## Contact
- Trade Portal: etrade.gov.et
- Phone: Check portal for contacts

documents.title: Digital Payment For Electricity Bills Ad005Ceb
documents.content:
Ethiopian Electric Utility (EEU) encourages customers to switch from cash‑based payments to digital payment options. This modernisation improves efficiency and reduces costs.
Key points:
- Digital transition: The EEU is shifting to digital payment methods to enhance transparency and save time, energy and costs.
- Digital Ethiopia initiative: The national ‘Digital Ethiopia’ programme aims to digitise service delivery across institutions by 2025.
- Payment options: Post‑paid meter customers can pay monthly electricity bills using TeleBirr, CBE Birr, mobile banking and internet banking.
- Roll‑out: Since May 2023, post‑paid customers in Addis Ababa pay through digital options. A nationwide roll‑out is planned for July.

documents.title: Emergency Services Contact Numbers E88E2Cc5
documents.content: Addis Ababa residents should call the Police Commission emergency numbers 991 or 922 for police emergencies. The fire brigade can be reached at 939, while the traffic police respond at 945; ambulance services run by the Ethiopian Red Cross Society in Addis Ababa can be reached at 907【26178437295987†L849-L852】.

documents.title: Ethiotelecom Apn And Volte Configuration C81330C9
documents.content:
APN configuration for 5G/4G internet:
- Create a new Access Point Name with Name: Ethio Telecom and APN: internet.
- MCC: 636, MNC: 01 and APN type: default,supl.
- On an Android device go to Settings → Connections → Mobile networks → Access point names, add a new APN with these values and restart.
VoLTE activation:
- Requirements: a 4G/5G compatible device with VoLTE capability, latest SIM card and updated software.
- Activation steps: Settings → Connections → Mobile networks → enable VoLTE calls or Enhanced 4G LTE Mode; restart if prompted.
documents.chunk_text:

- MCC: 636, MNC: 01 and APN type: default,supl.
- On an Android device go to Settings → Connections → Mobile networks → Access point names, add a new APN with these values and restart.
VoLTE activation:
- Requirements: a 4G/5G compatible device with VoLTE capability, latest SIM card and updated software.
- Activation steps: Settings → Connections → Mobile networks → enable VoLTE calls or Enhanced 4G LTE Mode; restart if prompted.

documents.title: Ethiotelecom Ussd Codes 91E74235
documents.content:
Ethiotelecom provides a variety of USSD codes and short numbers that allow subscribers to access services directly from their phones.
USSD codes:
- *127# – access TeleBirr mobile money.
- *804# – check account balance.
- *805# – recharge airtime for yourself or others.
- *806# – transfer airtime to friends or family.
- *807# – send a ‘call me back’ request.
- *809# – access the USSD services menu.
- *810# – access credit services and information.
- *822# – caller ring back tone (CRBT) services.
- *994# – SMS channel.
- *999# – Ethio Gebeta services.
Short numbers:
- 822 – CRBT services.
- 824 – voicemail services.
- 845, 886, 887 – voice SMS services (listen/send).
- 903 – EVDO & CDMA top‑up.
- 909 – check balance.
- 994 – general customer service.
- 980 – premium customer service.
- 700 – TeleBirr customer service.

documents.title: Marriage Registration Service
documents.content:
# Marriage Registration Service - Addis Ababa

## Overview
Marriage registration is the official recording of marriage with the Civil Registration and Residency Services Agency (CRRSA). Both civil and traditional/religious marriages should be registered.

## Where to Register
Visit the Civil Registration and Residency Services Agency branch office in your woreda.

## Eligibility Criteria
- Both parties must be 18 years or older
- Neither party can have an existing marriage
- Both parties must appear in person

## Required Documents
1. Birth certificates of both spouses (if available)
2. Two 4x4 photographs taken within the last 6 months
3. Divorce certificate (if previously married)
4. Proof of residence or national ID
5. Witnesses (if required)

## Marriage Notice Period
- Registrar posts notice for 15 consecutive days
- Objections can be submitted within this period

## Important Rules
- Minimum age: 18 years for both parties
- Both parties must appear in person
- Traditional/religious marriages can be registered

## Contact Information
- Phone: 7533
- Email: info@aacrrsa.gov.et

documents.title: Marriage Registration Service And Certificate 06Dce496
documents.content:
The marriage registration service is available at the woreda level through the Civil Registration and Residency Services Agency. Couples must notify the registrar and meet specific rules before receiving a certificate.
Eligibility:
- Notification period: Couples must notify the registrar at least one month before the intended marriage date (not required for religious or traditional marriages).
- Public notice: After the interview, the registrar posts a notice stating the marriage date for fifteen days.
- Objections: Written objections can be filed within fifteen days by parents, guardians, prosecutors or anyone claiming a prior marriage; the registrar must decide within five days.
- Minimum age: Neither man nor woman may marry before age 18.
Required documents:
1. Identity documents – valid residence or national ID card, passport, defence‑force ID, immigration/residence permit or residency letter. Foreign citizens of Ethiopian origin must present a foreign citizen ID.
2. Residence evidence for family weddings – if the marriage is held at a parent’s or relative’s home, the parents’ residence/national ID or a residency letter is required.
3. Workplace marriages – proof from the employer that the couple works and lives at the camp/project in the registration office’s area.
4. Witness identification – witnesses must provide residence or national ID, passport or defence‑force ID.
5. Divorce certificate – required if the groom was previously married.
6. Photographs – two recent 3×4 cm photographs of the couple.
7. Witness appearance – for marriages already performed by religious or traditional ceremony, witnesses or a knowledgeable person must appear before the registrar and sign.
8. Birth certificates – optional but should be submitted if available.
Notes: Residence IDs are not issued or renewed by proxy and diaspora Ethiopians and foreign nationals living abroad do not receive residence IDs.

documents.title: Residence Id Service
documents.content:
# Residence ID - Addis Ababa

## Overview
Residence ID is an official identification card issued to residents of Addis Ababa by the Civil Registration and Residency Services Agency (CRRSA).

## Where to Apply
Visit the Civil Registration and Residency Services Agency headquarters or branch offices.

## Eligibility
- Must be 18 years or older
- Must be registered as a resident in the woreda
- Must appear in person (cannot apply by proxy)

## Required Documents
1. Completed residence registration form
2. Two 4x4 passport photos (taken within 6 months)
3. Service request form

## Important Notes
- Residence ID is valid for the period specified on the card
- ID must be renewed before expiration
- Cannot be issued at business houses
- Diasporas and foreign nationals cannot get residence ID
- ID cannot be obtained through proxy

## Renewal
- Submit application before expiration
- Appear in person
- Provide updated photos if needed

## Contact
- Phone: 7533
- Email: info@aacrrsa.gov.et

documents.title: Water Bill Payment Via Mobile Apps 6598D27C
documents.content: Water utility bills can be paid through the WeBirr platform using various mobile services. To pay with the Telebirr mobile application: open the Telebirr app, tap the Payments tab, choose the Service Payment or Utilities option, select WeBirr or Water Utility by WeBirr, enter your payment code and confirm the payment【339569026563821†L15-L23】. For the CBE Birr Plus app: open the app, tap Pay, select Pay Bill or Water Supply, choose WeBirr or your water supply city, enter your consumer code and confirm【339569026563821†L26-L33】. Payments can also be made via USSD: dial *847# on CBE Birr or *127# on Telebirr, choose Pay Bill and WeBirr, enter the payment code and confirm【339569026563821†L59-L65】.
